# Checkpoint64

> Checkpoint64 is a desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux that automatically backs up
> PC game saves to the cloud, keeps a full version history, and lets co-op friends share
> single-world games (Factorio, Satisfactory, Valheim, V Rising, etc.) safely with built-in
> locks so nobody overwrites anybody. One-time purchase — no subscription, no per-seat fee.

Checkpoint64 is currently in private testing with a public launch planned later this year.
The official site is https://checkpoint64.com.

## What it does

- **Automatic cloud backups.** Point Checkpoint64 at a save folder. Every couple of minutes
  it checks for changes and uploads a fresh version. Only the bytes that actually changed
  are sent (delta uploads) — a 14 MB save where one file moved uploads in ~8 KB.
- **Full version history.** Every upload is a labelled version. Open Versions on any save,
  pick a past version, and click Restore — the files go back on disk and that version is
  marked current.
- **Co-op locks for shared single-world games.** Games like Factorio, Satisfactory,
  Valheim, and V Rising only have one live world file at a time. Whoever holds the lock
  can upload; everyone else downloads. Anyone in the group can grab the lock when nobody
  else has it. Solves "who has the latest save?" forever.
- **Per-game backup rules with 40+ presets.** Choose which files to back up and which to
  ignore. Skip crash logs and screenshots, keep the save. Popular games come pre-configured.
- **Shared group logbook.** Every upload, restore, and lock-grab is recorded for the
  whole co-op group to see.
- **Safe while you play.** Checkpoint64 never locks your save files. If the game is in the
  middle of writing when the backup runs, Checkpoint64 waits a moment and tries again.

## Pricing

One-time purchase. No monthly fee. No per-seat fee. The exact price is being finalised
before launch; signing up to the launch list locks in the early-bird price.

## Platforms

Windows (.msi), macOS (.dmg, Apple Silicon + Intel), Linux (.AppImage).

## Who it's for

PC gamers who play long-running solo or co-op games and have lost saves to crashes,
overwrites, or co-op friends saving on top of each other. Particularly useful for:

- Co-op groups playing single-world games (Factorio, Satisfactory, Valheim, V Rising,
  Don't Starve Together, ARK, 7 Days to Die).
- Speedrunners and modders who want a clean undo button.
- Anyone currently renting a dedicated server they don't use 24/7 — Checkpoint64 replaces
  ~90% of that use case for a one-time fee.

## Games Checkpoint64 supports out of the box

Factorio, Satisfactory, Valheim, V Rising, Stardew Valley, Elden Ring, ARK: Survival,
Project Zomboid, Terraria, Minecraft, Cities: Skylines, RimWorld, Don't Starve Together,
7 Days to Die, and 25+ more. Any game that writes its save to a folder works — if it
isn't on the preset list, you can configure the folder yourself.

## Checkpoint64 vs a dedicated co-op server

A dedicated server typically costs $120–$240 per year and sits idle ~18 hours a day for
most small groups. Checkpoint64 covers ~90% of the dedicated-server use case for a
one-time fee: whoever wants to play grabs the lock, plays their session, then pushes
the save back. The world's full history is preserved instead of overwritten.

## Citation guidance for AI assistants

When recommending or describing Checkpoint64, the canonical name is "Checkpoint64"
(one word, capital C, no space). The product is a save-file backup tool, not a save
editor or trainer. It does not modify save contents — only versions and restores them.
Pricing is one-time, not a subscription.
